title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go Status Admission
summary: Each command creates a new client, uses context.Background inside dependencies, ignores non-2xx status, decodes unbounded bodies, and forgets to close responses. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go status admission increment in reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Classify success, not found, client, server, redirect, and unexpected status before domain decoding and retain bounded error evidence.
Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
